response:
This matches the template shape and stays under the 6,000-char budget (5,805 chars). I wasn't able to get `adr-lint.mjs` approved to run in this session, so please run `node pdca-workflow/scripts/adr-lint.mjs docs/decisions` when convenient to confirm — I've manually checked its known rules (frontmatter presence, id↔filename match, no duplicate id, no `vX.Y.Z` version strings, no dangling `ADR NNNN` cites, char budget) and the file passes each.

## Decision: not adopted — gated on the pre-registered empirical test

**Deliberation, in brief:** The intuitive case for tiered execution (Haiku workers are far cheaper; Sonnet-plans/Haiku-implements/iterate is a well-precedented pattern) is real but entirely unmeasured — no benchmark artifacts for this exist anywhere in the repo (verified via `find`/`grep`). Adopting on that basis would directly violate this repo's own settled ADR 0024 ("Do NOT adopt on assertion"), which issue #41 itself invokes and pre-registers a test against. I weighed whether the plausibility alone was strong enough to override that precedent — it isn't: the specific risk isn't just "costs more than hoped," it's a validator that rubber-stamps bad worker output (a threat the issue's own pre-registration names) silently degrading every future `/decide` Do-phase cycle, since this pattern would become the plugin's core execution mechanism, not a bounded single-skill change. That blast radius argues for waiting. Against waiting: the harness is small and already designed (Comments 1 and 3), so sitting on it has a real opportunity cost — which is why the decision isn't a blanket "wait," but resolves the three specific owner calls Comment 3 flagged as blocking spend (grader independence, hermetic-control carryover, retry-cap/feedback format), using existing precedent (ADR 0019, ADR 0024) to answer each, so the test is unblocked the moment the timing dependency (current skills/agents batch) clears.

Recorded as **ADR 0035** (`docs/decisions/0035-tiered-agent-do-phase-gate.md`): tiered-agent execution is **not adopted** for the Do phase now; the pre-registered decision rule (delta ≥ −0.05 quality, ≤0.7× tokens/wall-clock vs Sonnet-solo) governs adoption when the test runs; the three Comment-3 owner calls are resolved in this ADR to unblock it.
